FIRST check docs/learned/index.md for existing documentation on this topic. Read relevant docs before exploring raw files. Then explore codebase as necessary.
I need to understand the following files and patterns for implementing "Convert erk launch to @no_repo_required with --repo flag":
1. Read `src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py` - the main launch command 2. Read `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/abc.py` - RemoteGitHub ABC 3. Read `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/real.py` - Real implementation 4. Read `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/fake.py` - Fake implementation 5. Read `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/types.py` - existing types (if it exists) 6. Read `src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py` - for repo_option, resolve_owner_repo, get_remote_github patterns 7. Read `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py` - as pattern reference for is_remote routing 8. Read `tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py` - as pattern reference for remote test structure 9. Read `tests/commands/launch/test_launch_cmd.py` - existing launch tests for context
For each file, provide the FULL contents. This is critical for implementation.

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: No-Repo Infrastructure 3→read_when: 4→ - "adding a command that works outside git repositories" 5→ - "using NoRepoSentinel or @no_repo_required" 6→ - "implementing remote dispatch without local repo context" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "accessing ctx.git or ctx.repo without checking for NoRepoSentinel" 9→ warning: "Commands decorated with @no_repo_required may have sentinel gateways. Check isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) before repo operations." 10→--- 11→ 12→# No-Repo Infrastructure 13→ 14→Erk commands normally require a git repository context. The no-repo infrastructure allows specific commands to run outside repositories by providing sentinel gateways that raise user-friendly errors instead of crashing. 15→ 16→## NoRepoSentinel Pattern 17→ 18→Sentinel gateways implement the same ABC interfaces as real gateways but raise `NoRepoError` with guidance when called. This means commands can be wired up normally — the error only fires if the command actually tries to use repo-dependent functionality. 19→ 20→## `@no_repo_required` Decorator 21→ 22→Commands decorated with `@no_repo_required` skip repository detection during context construction. Instead of failing at startup when no repo is found, the context is built with sentinel gateways for repo-dependent services. 23→ 24→## RemoteGitHub Consolidation 25→ 26→<!-- Source: src/erk/cli/commands/one_shot.py --> 27→ 28→The one-shot dispatch command demonstrates the full no-repo pattern. It supports both local repo mode and remote-only mode via `--repo owner/name`: 29→ 30→### Context Injection Pattern
... [25 lines omitted] ...
35→2. If no `ctx.http_client` available, raise `UserFacingCliError` 42→### Error Handling

1→--- 2→read_when: 3→ - considering invoke_without_command=True for Click command groups 4→ - deciding between command groups vs separate commands for local/remote workflows 5→ - understanding why erk uses separate commands instead of unified groups 6→title: Local/Remote Command Group Pattern (Deprecated) 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: using this pattern 9→ warning: 10→ "BEFORE: Using invoke_without_command=True to unify local/remote variants 11→ → READ: Why this pattern was abandoned" 12→--- 13→ 14→# Local/Remote Command Group Pattern (Deprecated) 15→ 16→**Historical pattern for unified local/remote command execution in Click. No longer used in erk.** 17→ 18→## Why This Pattern Was Abandoned 19→ 20→Click's `invoke_without_command=True` allows a group to execute when called directly (local) while offering subcommands (remote). This seems elegant but created operational friction: 21→ 22→**The fundamental problem:** Local and remote execution have different preconditions, options, and error modes. Unifying them under one command name obscured these differences. 23→ 24→### Concrete Issues 25→ 26→1. **Help text ambiguity** — Users running `erk pr address --help` saw both local and remote documentation mixed together, making it unclear which flags applied to which mode 27→ 28→2. **Option validation complexity** — The group function needed conditional logic to validate options based on whether a subcommand was invoked (`ctx.invoked_subcommand is None`), spreading validation across multiple functions 29→ 30→3. **Discoverability** — Remote execution via `erk pr address remote` was non-obvious compared to the explicit `erk launch pr-address` pattern
... [67 lines omitted] ...

Remote mode has no local git/gh CLI." 11→--- 12→ 13→# RemoteGitHub Gateway 14→ 15→REST API-based GitHub operations without a local git or `gh` CLI dependency. 1→"""Abstract interface for remote GitHub operations without a local git clone. 2→ 3→This gateway enables GitHub operations (branch creation, file commits, PR management, 4→workflow dispatch) using the GitHub REST API directly, without requiring a local 5→git repository. All methods take explicit owner/repo parameters. 6→""" 7→ 8→from abc import ABC, abstractmethod 9→ 10→from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueInfo, IssueNotFound, PRReference 11→ 12→ 13→class RemoteGitHub(ABC): 14→ """GitHub operations that don't require a local git clone. 15→ 16→ Every method takes explicit owner/repo parameters instead of repo_root: Path. 17→ Uses the GitHub REST API via HttpClient. 18→ """ 19→ 20→ @abstractmethod 21→ def get_authenticated_user(self) -> str: 22→ """Get the username of the authenticated GitHub user. 23→ 24→ Returns: 25→ GitHub username string 26→ """ 27→ ... 28→ 29→ @abstractmethod 30→ def get_default_branch_name(self, *, owner: str, repo: str) -> str:

Run 'erk init' to create it.") 20→ 21→ result = discover_repo_or_sentinel(start, ctx.global_config.erk_root, ctx.git) 22→ if isinstance(result, RepoContext): 23→ return result 24→ raise FileNotFoundError(result.message) 25→ 26→ 27→def worktree_path_for(worktrees_dir: Path, name: str) -> Path: 28→ """Return the absolute path for a named worktree within worktrees directory. 29→ 30→ Note: Does not handle 'root' as a special case. session.assistant:
Perfect! Now I have enough context. Let me create a comprehensive analysis report:
## Implementation Analysis Report: Convert `erk launch` to @no_repo_required with --repo flag
---
## Overview
The task is to convert the `erk launch` command from requiring a local git repository to supporting the `@no_repo_required` pattern (allowing execution outside repos via `--repo owner/repo` flag).
---
## Current Architecture
### Launch Command (`/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py`)
**Lines 352-531: Main `launch()` function**
Currently: - **Requires local repo**: Lines 462-467 check `Ensure.invariant(not isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel), "Not in a git repository")` - **Uses `ctx.github`**: LocalGitHub gateway that requires a local git repo (uses `gh` CLI) - **Gets PR details**: Calls `ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, pr_number)` which needs repo context
Key dispatch handlers: - `_dispatch_pr_rebase()` (lines 75-141): Gets PR via local github, needs branch name from current repo - `_dispatch_pr_address()` (lines 144-190): Gets PR via local github - `_dispatch_pr_rewrite()` (lines 193-242): Gets PR via local github - `_dispatch_learn()` (lines 245-274): Uses local github for metadata, gets repo slug from `repo.github` - `_dispatch_one_shot()` (lines 297-349): Gets PR and calls `ctx.github.check_auth_status()`
---
## Pattern Reference: `erk pr dispatch`
**File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py`
**Pattern to emulate** (lines 844-921):
```python @click.command("dispatch") @click.argument(...) @repo_option # ← Key: Uses repo_option decorator @click.pass_obj def pr_dispatch(ctx, ..., target_repo: str | None) -> None: # Remote mode: --repo flag or no local git repo is_remote = target_repo is not None or isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel)
if is_remote: _dispatch_remote(ctx, ...) # Uses RemoteGitHub else: _dispatch_local(ctx, ...) # Uses LocalGitHub ```
**Key infrastructure used**: - `repo_option` from `src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py` (line 75-82) - `resolve_owner_repo()` to parse owner/repo format - `get_remote_github()` to construct RemoteGitHub instance - Remote dispatch handler: `_dispatch_planned_pr_plan_remote()` (lines 443-593)
---
## Key Gateway Classes
### RemoteGitHub ABC **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/abc.py`
**Supports**: - `get_authenticated_user()` - Returns username - `get_default_branch_name(owner, repo)` - Gets default branch - `dispatch_workflow(owner, repo, workflow, ref, inputs)` - Dispatches workflows - `get_issue(owner, repo, number)` - Fetch issue/PR details
**Does NOT support**: - Any local filesystem operations - PR fetch with implicit repo context
### RealRemoteGitHub Implementation **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/real.py`
**Key methods**: - `dispatch_workflow()` (lines 150-203): Uses distinct_id polling pattern - `get_issue()` (lines 222-237): Returns IssueInfo or IssueNotFound - `check_auth_status()` (lines 387-396): Checks REST API authentication
### FakeRemoteGitHub **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/remote_github/fake.py`
Test doubles with: - Constructor-injected read responses (issues, comments, dispatch_run_id) - Mutation tracking lists for write assertions (created_refs, dispatched_workflows, etc.)
---
## Infrastructure Functions
### `resolve_owner_repo()` **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py` (lines 15-46)
Validates `owner/repo` format or extracts from local git.
### `get_remote_github()` **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py` (lines 49-72)
Returns RemoteGitHub instance: 1. Uses ctx.remote_github if provided (test injection) 2. Constructs RealRemoteGitHub from ctx.http_client if available 3. Raises UserFacingCliError if no http_client
### `repo_option` **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py` (lines 75-82)
Click decorator that adds `--repo owner/repo` option.
---
## NoRepoSentinel Pattern
**Type**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/context/types.py` (lines 135-144)
```python @dataclass(frozen=True) class NoRepoSentinel: """Sentinel value indicating execution outside a git repository.""" message: str = "Not inside a git repository" ```
**Guard pattern** (from `launch_cmd.py` line 463): ```python Ensure.invariant( not isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel), "Not in a git repository", ) ```
Must be changed to allow both local and remote execution.
---
## Test Patterns
### Remote path tests **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py` (lines 1-100+)
Pattern for testing `--repo` support: ```python def _build_remote_context( fake_remote: FakeRemoteGitHub, target_repo: str | None = "owner/repo", ) -> context_for_test: """Build ErkContext configured for remote mode testing.""" return context_for_test( repo=NoRepoSentinel(), remote_github=fake_remote, )
def test_view_remote_displays_plan() -> None: issue = _make_issue(42, title="Remote Plan Title") fake_remote = _make_fake_remote(issues={42: issue}) ctx = _build_remote_context(fake_remote)
runner = CliRunner() result = runner.invoke(cli, ["pr", "view", "42", "--repo", "owner/repo"], obj=ctx)
assert result.exit_code == 0 ```
### Launch command tests **File**: `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/tests/commands/launch/test_launch_cmd.py` (lines 1-300+)
Current tests only cover local mode with FakeLocalGitHub.
---
## Implementation Strategy
### Phase 1: Add `--repo` option and branching logic
1. Import `repo_option` from `repo_resolution.py` 2. Add `@repo_option` decorator to `launch()` function 3. Add `target_repo: str | None` parameter 4. Add branching at start of `launch()`: ```python is_remote = target_repo is not None or isinstance(ctx.repo, NoRepoSentinel) if is_remote: _dispatch_remote(...) else: _dispatch_local(...) ```
### Phase 2: Create local dispatch handler
Extract current `launch()` logic into `_dispatch_local()`: - All current dispatch handlers become local-only - Keep all checks that require local repo
### Phase 3: Create remote dispatch handler
New `_dispatch_remote()` function: - Uses `resolve_owner_repo()` to validate owner/repo - Uses `get_remote_github()` to get RemoteGitHub instance - For each workflow type: - Use `remote.get_issue(owner, repo, pr_number)` instead of `ctx.github.get_pr()` - Use `remote.dispatch_workflow()` instead of `ctx.github.trigger_workflow()` - Handle `IssueNotFound` instead of `PRNotFound`
### Phase 4: Remote dispatch helpers
Create remote-specific handler functions: - `_dispatch_pr_rebase_remote()` - Fetch PR via remote, dispatch workflow - `_dispatch_pr_address_remote()` - Same pattern - `_dispatch_pr_rewrite_remote()` - Same pattern - `_dispatch_one_shot_remote()` - Same pattern - `_dispatch_learn_remote()` - Simpler, no PR fetch needed
Key differences from local: - No branch checkout needed - No `ctx.github.check_auth_status()` (use `remote.get_authenticated_user()`) - Return `IssueNotFound` instead of `PRNotFound` for type consistency
### Phase 5: Add tests
Create `tests/commands/launch/test_launch_remote_paths.py`: - Test each workflow with `--repo owner/repo` flag - Test error when `--repo` format is invalid - Test error when PR not found in remote - Test successful workflow dispatch via REST API
---
## Critical Differences: Local vs Remote
| Aspect | Local | Remote | |--------|-------|--------| | **PR Fetch** | `ctx.github.get_pr(repo.root, pr_number)` returns `PRDetails` | `remote.get_issue(owner, repo, pr_number)` returns `IssueInfo \| IssueNotFound` | | **Auth** | `ctx.github.check_auth_status()` | `remote.get_authenticated_user()` or check `check_auth_status()` | | **Workflow Dispatch** | `ctx.github.trigger_workflow(repo_root, workflow, inputs, ref)` | `remote.dispatch_workflow(owner, repo, workflow, ref, inputs)` | | **Repo Slug** | From `repo.github` in RepoContext | Passed explicitly as `owner/repo` | | **Default Branch** | Context or detection | `remote.get_default_branch_name(owner, repo)` | | **Mutations** | None - only dispatches | Same - only dispatches |
---
## Key Implementation Details
### Type Consistency - RemoteGitHub returns `IssueInfo` (has `.title`, `.url`, `.labels`, `.body`) - LocalGitHub returns `PRDetails` (has same fields) - Ensure handlers use consistent field names
### Error Handling - `resolve_owner_repo()` raises `UserFacingCliError` if format invalid - `get_remote_github()` raises `UserFacingCliError` if no http_client - `remote.get_issue()` returns `IssueNotFound` sentinel - check with `isinstance()`
### Authentication - Remote mode requires `ctx.http_client` (gh CLI authenticated) - `get_remote_github()` validates this and raises if missing
### Workflow Dispatch Pattern From `dispatch_cmd.py` lines 507-528: ```python dispatch_ref = ( ref if ref is not None else remote.get_default_branch_name(owner=owner, repo=repo_name) ) inputs = { "plan_id": str(plan_number), "submitted_by": submitted_by, "plan_title": validated.title, ... } run_id = remote.dispatch_workflow( owner=owner, repo=repo_name, workflow=DISPATCH_WORKFLOW_NAME, ref=dispatch_ref, inputs=inputs, ) ```
---
## Code Locations Summary
| Component | File Path | Key Lines | |-----------|-----------|-----------| | Current launch command | `src/erk/cli/commands/launch_cmd.py` | 352-531 | | RemoteGitHub ABC | `packages/erk-shared/.../remote_github/abc.py` | 1-393 | | RemoteGitHub real impl | `packages/erk-shared/.../remote_github/real.py` | 1-459 | | RemoteGitHub fake impl | `packages/erk-shared/.../remote_github/fake.py` | 1-470 | | Repo resolution helpers | `src/erk/cli/repo_resolution.py` | 1-82 | | PR dispatch pattern | `src/erk/cli/commands/pr/dispatch_cmd.py` | 629-921 | | Remote tests pattern | `tests/commands/pr/test_remote_paths.py` | 1-100+ | | Launch tests | `tests/commands/launch/test_launch_cmd.py` | 1-300+ | | NoRepoSentinel type | `packages/erk-shared/.../context/types.py` | 135-144 |
---
## Dependencies to Import
For `launch_cmd.py` modifications: ```python from erk.cli.repo_resolution import get_remote_github, repo_option, resolve_owner_repo from erk_shared.gateway.github.issues.types import IssueNotFound from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.abc import RemoteGitHub from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel ```
For tests: ```python from erk_shared.gateway.remote_github.fake import FakeRemoteGitHub from erk_shared.context.types import NoRepoSentinel from tests.fakes.context import context_for_test ```
---
## Notes for Implementation
1. **Ensure.invariant() guard**: Line 463 must be removed; replace repo requirement check with branching logic 2. **PR not found handling**: Change from `PRNotFound` to `IssueNotFound` 3. **Auth status**: Remote mode should use `remote.get_authenticated_user()` instead of `ctx.github.check_auth_status()` 4. **Repo slug construction**: In remote mode, build from `owner/repo` directly; in local mode, use `repo.github.owner/repo.github.repo` 5. **Default branch detection**: In remote mode, use `remote.get_default_branch_name()` instead of local git detection 6. **Test context**: Use `NoRepoSentinel()` + `remote_github=FakeRemoteGitHub(...)` for remote tests
---
This comprehensive analysis provides the complete context needed to implement the conversion from requiring a local git repository to supporting the `@no_repo_required` pattern with the `--repo` flag.
